requirements.full_sentence:
If not managed effectively, credit risk may have ramifications for a range of other risk
categories too.
requirements.object: range of other risk categories too

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks -
Credit Risk Management) Directions, 2025
2.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Credit Risk Management) Directions, 2025 2

requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into effect immediately upon issuance.
requirements.object: upon issuance

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Local Area Banks (hereinafter collectively
referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank').
requirements.object: Area Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank')

requirements.full_sentence:
(1) In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires,

Explanation: Exposure shall mean all fund-based and non-fund-based exposures.

5[(iiia) 'Entity' in the context of a 'related party' prescribed in Chapter IIIA of these
Directions shall mean a 'person' other than an individual and a Hindu Undivided
Family (HUF).]
Note: For the purpose of assessing the effectiveness of hedge, guidance may be
taken from the applicable accounting standards and the relevant guidance notes of
the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India on the matter.
requirements.object: than an individual and a Hindu Undivided Family (HUF)

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: An exposure shall be considered as naturally hedged only if the
offsetting exposure has the maturity / cash flow within the same accounting year.
requirements.object: hedged only if the offsetting exposure has the maturity / cash flow within the same accounting year

requirements.full_sentence:
For instance, export revenues (booked as receivable) may offset the exchange risk
arising out of repayment obligations of an external commercial borrowing if both
the exposures have cash flows / maturity within the same accounting year.
requirements.object: arising out of repayment obligations of an external commercial borrowing if both the exposures have cash flows / maturity wit

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) 'Bank Guarantee' shall mean financial and performance guarantees issued by
banks on behalf of their clients.
requirements.object: guarantees issued by banks on behalf of their clients

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) 'Bills Purchased and Discounted' shall mean negotiable instruments that give the
holder the right to receive stated fixed sums on demand or at a fixed or
determinable future time.
requirements.object: give the holder the right to receive stated fixed sums on demand or at a fixed or determinable future time

requirements.full_sentence:
1[(iia) 'Cash credit (CC)' shall mean a facility, under which a customer is allowed an
advance up to the credit limit against the security by way of hypothecation / pledge
of goods, book debts, standing crops, etc.
requirements.object: which a customer is allowed an advance up to the credit limit against the security by way of hypothecation / pledge of goods, b

requirements.full_sentence:
The outstanding amount is repayable on demand.]
2[(iiaa) 'Committee on lending to related parties' shall mean a committee of the Board
of the bank entrusted with sanctioning of loans to related parties.
requirements.object: the Board of the bank entrusted with sanctioning of loans to related parties

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks may also
identify any existing Committee, other than the Audit Committee, for this purpose.
requirements.object: Committee, other than the Audit Committee, for this purpose

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(iiab) 'Contract or arrangement' shall have the same meaning as specified in Section
188(1)(a) to (g) of the Companies Act, 2013.]
(iiac) 'Control' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under Section 2(27) of
the Companies Act, 2013.]
3[(iib) 'Current Account' shall mean a form of demand deposit account wherefrom
withdrawals are allowed any number of times depending upon the balance in the
account or up to a particular agreed amount and shall also be deemed to include
other deposit accounts which are neither Savings nor Term deposit account.]
4[(iic) 'Director of a bank' shall have the same meaning as defined in Explanation (b)
to Section 20 of the Banking Regulation Act 1949 and would include a nominee
director and an independent director.]
requirements.object: as specified in Section 188(1)(a) to (g) of the Companies Act, 2013

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) 'Entity' in the context of 'Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure' prescribed in
Chapter-IV of these Directions shall mean a counterparty to which a bank has
exposure in any currency.
requirements.object: which a bank has exposure in any currency

requirements.full_sentence:
9[A bank shall put in place a comprehensive Board approved policy on Credit Risk
Management.
requirements.object: comprehensive Board approved policy on Credit Risk Management

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall, inter alia, cover aspects related to lending to related
parties, unhedged foreign currency exposures and valuation of properties
including empanelment of valuers.
requirements.object: aspects related to lending to related parties, unhedged foreign currency exposures and valuation of properties including empanelme

requirements.full_sentence:
Credit assessments carried out by a bank shall suitably factor in the possible impact of calamities on borrowers who may be impacted by such events.]

requirements.object: possible impact of calamities on borrowers who may be impacted by such events

requirements.full_sentence:
In terms of Section 20(1)(b) of the BR Act, 1949, a bank is prohibited from entering
into any commitment for granting any loans or advances to or on behalf of any of
its directors, or any firm in which any of its directors is interested as partner,
manager, employee or guarantor, or any company [not being a subsidiary of the
banking company or a company registered under Section 8 of the Companies Act,
2013, or a Government company] of which, or the subsidiary or the holding
company of which any of the directors of the bank is a director, managing agent,
manager, employee or guarantor or in which they hold substantial interest, or any
individual in respect of whom any of its directors is a partner or guarantor.
requirements.object: commitment for granting any loans or advances to or on behalf of any of its directors, or any firm in which any of its director

Provided that, till the director relinquishes the directorship of either the bank or the
company, the bank shall not further renew such a facility on or after its contracted

requirements.object: facility on or after its contracted 11Deleted with effect from April 01, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Cred

requirements.full_sentence:
The interest rate charged on all such loans shall not
be lower than the rate charged to the employees.
requirements.object: rate charged to the employees

requirements.full_sentence:
(v) Non-Fund Based (NFB) facility on behalf of a director or his / her related party,
provided that all such facilities shall be fully secured by cash collateral of equivalent
or higher value.
requirements.object: cash collateral of equivalent or higher value

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, cash collateral would not be mandatory in exposures arising on
account of derivative transactions.]
13[B.1 *****
requirements.object: not be mandatory in exposures arising on account of derivative transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
In terms of Section 20A(2) of the Act ibid, any remission made in contravention of
the provisions of sub-section (1) above shall be void and of no effect.
requirements.object: no effect

requirements.full_sentence:
While granting loans and advances against shares, statutory provisions contained
in Sections 19(2) and 19(3) of the BR Act, 1949 shall be strictly observed.

Therefore, a bank shall
not provide loans to companies for buy-back of shares / securities.
requirements.object: for buy-back of shares / securities

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall have the overall responsibility of ensuring that suitable mechanisms
are put in place for implementation of the policy on lending to related parties by the
bank.
requirements.object: of ensuring that suitable mechanisms are put in place for implementation of the policy on lending to related parties b

requirements.full_sentence:
The credit policy (hereinafter called the policy) of a bank, as required in terms of the
extant directions, shall contain specific provisions relating to 'lending to related parties'
in accordance with the provisions of these Directions.
requirements.object: to 'lending to related parties' in accordance with the provisions of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall prescribe, inter
alia, additional safeguards to address the risks emanating from lending to related
parties.
requirements.object: safeguards to address the risks emanating from lending to related parties

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall also have specific provisions for lending to 'specified employees' of
the bank and their relatives.
requirements.object: for lending to 'specified employees' of the bank and their relatives
of the whistleblowing mechanism, encourage employees to communicate confidentially and without the risk of reprisal, legitimate concerns

requirements.full_sentence: The policy shall specify aggregate limits for loans towards related parties.
requirements.object: loans towards related parties

requirements.full_sentence:
Within this
aggregate limit, there shall be sub-limits for loans to a single related party and a group
of related parties.
requirements.object: to a single related party and a group of related parties

requirements.full_sentence:
These limits shall be within the extant prudential exposure limits
prescribed by the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: prudential exposure limits prescribed by the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Provisions of paragraph 7 in Chapter III shall also apply to grant of loans and
advances to spouse and minor / dependent children of the directors of banks.
requirements.object: of loans and advances to spouse and minor / dependent children of the directors of banks

requirements.full_sentence:
However, banks may grant loan or advance to or on behalf of spouses of their directors
in cases where the spouse has his / her own independent source of income arising
out of his / her employment or profession and the facility so granted is based on
standard procedures and norms for assessing the creditworthiness of the borrower.
requirements.object: to or on behalf of spouses of their directors in cases where the spouse has his / her own independent source of income arising o

requirements.full_sentence:
Such facility should be extended on commercial terms, conforming to arms-length
principle.
requirements.object: terms, conforming to arms-length principle

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the restrictions placed on loans and advances by a bank to its directors
and the companies in which its directors are interested under Section 20 of the
Banking Regulation Act, 1949, a bank shall not have any exposure (including
investments in the equity / debt capital instruments) to its promoters and their relatives;
requirements.object: investments in the equity / debt capital instruments) to its promoters and their relatives

requirements.full_sentence:
Loans to related parties, which are not prohibited in terms of provisions of Chapter III
or this Chapter of these Directions, or which have been permitted under power
exercised by the Reserve Bank under clause (a) of the Explanation under sub-section
4 of Section 20 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, except (i) credit facilities fully
secured by cash or liquid securities and in accordance to prescribed LTV and
valuation norms for such securities, and (ii) interbank loans, shall be subject to a
materiality threshold as per the credit policy, which shall not be higher than ₹10 lakh.
requirements.object: lakh

requirements.full_sentence:
Materiality thresholds may vary for different categories of loans to related parties as
per the bank's policy.
requirements.object: of loans to related parties as per the bank's policy

requirements.full_sentence:
All loans above the prescribed materiality threshold shall be sanctioned either by the
Board or by the 'Committee on Lending to Related Parties' of the bank.
requirements.object: the Board or by the 'Committee on Lending to Related Parties' of the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Directors, KMP, or 'specified employees' shall recuse themselves from deliberations
and decision on loan proposals, or contracts and arrangements, involving themselves
or their related parties.
requirements.object: and decision on loan proposals, or contracts and arrangements, involving themselves or their related parties

requirements.full_sentence:
Such recusal shall also extend to deliberations and decisions
relating to any subsequent material changes to the terms of such loans, including one-
time settlements, write-offs, waivers, enforcement of security, implementation of
resolution plans, etc.
requirements.object: and decisions relating to any subsequent material changes to the terms of such loans, including one- time settlements, wr

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall put in place a suitable mechanism for maintaining and periodically
updating the list of all the related persons, and the related parties thereof, as well as
the loans sanctioned by the bank to such related persons and related parties.
requirements.object: suitable mechanism for maintaining and periodically updating the list of all the related persons, and the related parties thereof, as w

requirements.full_sentence:
Credit facilities sanctioned to 'specified employees' and their relatives shall be
reported to the Board on an annual basis.
requirements.object: Board on an annual basis

requirements.full_sentence:
Periodic reviews shall be conducted at quarterly or shorter intervals by internal
auditors to check, inter alia, whether guidelines and procedures in relation to loans to
related parties are being adhered to or not.
requirements.object: or shorter intervals by internal auditors to check, inter alia, whether guidelines and procedures in relation to loans to re

requirements.full_sentence:
Any deviation from the policy relating to lending to related parties and reasons
therefor such deviations shall be reported to the Audit Committee of the Board.
requirements.object: Audit Committee of the Board

requirements.full_sentence:
Any product, entity or structure formed with the objective of circumventing these
Directions through various means, such as reciprocal lending or quid pro quo
arrangements, and identified as such by the auditors of the bank or by the supervisory
authority and investigating agencies shall always be treated as lending to related
party.
requirements.object: lending to related party

requirements.full_sentence:
Any non-compliance with and circumvention of these Directions shall result in
supervisory and enforcement actions as deemed appropriate by the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: enforcement actions as deemed appropriate by the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
These actions may include imposition of monetary penalty, requirement of full
provisioning, directions to conduct staff accountability exercises, forensic audits, and
restrictions or any other supervisory and enforcement actions as deemed fit.]
<image>
requirements.object: penalty, requirement of full provisioning, directions to conduct staff accountability exercises, forensic audits, and r

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall assess and monitor the Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure
(UCFE) of entities and maintain adequate provisioning / capital for the same, as
per the instructions provided in this Chapter.
requirements.object: Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure (UCFE) of entities and maintain adequate provisioning / capital for the same, as per the ins

requirements.full_sentence:
Computation of UFCE
(1) A bank shall ascertain the Foreign Currency Exposure (FCE) of all entities at
least on an annual basis.
requirements.object: Exposure (FCE) of all entities at least on an annual basis

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall compute the FCE following the relevant
accounting standard applicable for the entity.
requirements.object: the relevant accounting standard applicable for the entity

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation:
Note: For arriving at the foreign currency exposure of entities, their exposure from
all sources including foreign currency borrowings and External Commercial
Borrowings shall be taken into account.

(2) A bank shall assess the Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure (UFCE) of
entities with FCE by obtaining information on UFCE from the concerned entity.
requirements.object: Currency Exposure (UFCE) of entities with FCE by obtaining information on UFCE from the concerned entity

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the information on UFCE shall be obtained from entities on a
quarterly basis based on statutory audit, internal audit, or self-declaration by the
concerned entity.
requirements.object: on a quarterly basis based on statutory audit, internal audit, or self-declaration by the concerned entity

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that UFCE information shall be audited and certified by the
statutory auditors of the entity, at least on an annual basis.
requirements.object: by the statutory auditors of the entity, at least on an annual basis

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: A bank shall use the data published by FEDAI on largest annual
volatility of USD-INR rate over a period of last 10 years and the same shall be
used for computation of potential loss by multiplying it with UFCE.
requirements.object: by FEDAI on largest annual volatility of USD-INR rate over a period of last 10 years and the same shall be used for computation

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: The UFCE in currencies other than USD shall be converted into USD using
the current market rates for determining the potential loss from UFCE.
requirements.object: using the current market rates for determining the potential loss from UFCE

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall determine the susceptibility of the entity to adverse exchange rate
movements by computing the ratio of the potential loss to entity from UFCE and
the entity's EBID over the last four quarters as per the latest quarterly results
certified by the statutory auditors.
requirements.object: the entity to adverse exchange rate movements by computing the ratio of the potential loss to entity from UFCE and the

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: (i) In cases where a bank is not in a position to obtain information on UFCE
or EBID from listed entities for the latest quarter due to restrictions on disclosure
of such information prior to finalisation of accounts, the bank shall have the option
to use data pertaining to the immediately preceding last four quarters for
computing capital and provisioning requirements.
requirements.object: use data pertaining to the immediately preceding last four quarters for computing capital and provisioning requirements

requirements.full_sentence:
Table 1: Incremental Provisioning and Capital Requirements for UFCE
Incremental Provisioning Requirement (basis
Potential Loss / EBID
Incremental Capital
(per cent)
Requirement
points)
Upto 15 per cent 0 0 More than 15 per cent and upto 30 per cent 20bps 0
More than 30 per cent and upto 50 per cent 40bps 0
More than 50 percent and upto 75 per cent 60bps 0
More than 75 per cent 80bps 25 per centage point increase in the risk
weight
Explanation:
Note: The incremental provisioning for UFCE shall be based on the total exposure
amount which is used for computing standard asset provisioning and the
incremental capital requirements for UFCE shall be based on the total exposure
amount which is used for computing credit risk capital requirements.
requirements.object: total exposure amount which is used for computing standard asset provisioning and the incremental capital requirements for UFCE shall

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) A bank shall calculate the incremental provisioning and capital requirements
Note: A banks shall put in place a system for information sharing and
dissemination in terms of Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Transfer
and Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: and capital requirements Note: A banks shall put in place a system for information sharing and dissemination in

requirements.full_sentence:
Exemption / Relaxation
A bank shall have the option to exclude the following exposures from the
calculation of UFCE:
(1) Exposures to entities classified as sovereign, banks, and individuals;
requirements.object: exclude the following exposures from the calculation of UFCE: (1) Exposures to entities classified as sovereign, banks, and individ

requirements.full_sentence:
For this purpose, 'sovereign' shall include domestic and foreign sovereign
as provided in Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential
Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: sovereign as provided in Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
For this purpose, the RBI regulated Financial Institutions, Bank of
International Settlement (BIS), International Monetary Fund (IMF) and
eligible Multilateral Development Banks (MDBs) listed in Reserve Bank of
India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy)
Directions, 2025 shall also be considered as 'bank'.
requirements.object: 'bank'

requirements.full_sentence:
and
Provided that the bank is satisfied that such foreign currency exposures are
appropriately hedged or managed robustly by the parent.
requirements.object: that such foreign currency exposures are appropriately hedged or managed robustly by the parent

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: For example, an Indian subsidiary of an MNC incorporated outside
India may have borrowed from its parent.
requirements.object: parent

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Exposures arising from derivative transactions and / or factoring transactions
Note: A bank shall compute the largest annual volatility over a period of last 10
years in the following manner: First, daily changes in the foreign exchange rates
shall be computed as a log return of today's rate over the previous day's rate.
requirements.object: volatility over a period of last 10 years in the following manner: First, daily changes in the foreign exchange rates shall

requirements.full_sentence:
Second, daily volatility shall be computed as standard deviation of these returns
over a period of one year (250 observations).
requirements.object: deviation of these returns over a period of one year (250 observations)

requirements.full_sentence:
Third, this daily volatility shall be
annualised by multiplying it by square root of 250.
requirements.object: it by square root of 250

requirements.full_sentence:
This computation shall be
performed on a daily basis for all the days in the last 10 years.
requirements.object: daily basis for all the days in the last 10 years

requirements.full_sentence:
The largest annual
volatility thus computed shall be used for the computation of the potential loss by
multiplying it with the UFCE.
requirements.object: computation of the potential loss by multiplying it with the UFCE

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that non-individual borrowers with aggregate exposure of ₹ 5
crore and above from banks (Scheduled Commercial Banks (excluding Regional
Rural Banks), Local Area Banks, Small Finance Banks, and Primary (Urban) Co-
operative Banks) and Financial Institutions (All India Financial Institutions and
NBFCs (including HFCs)) obtain LEI codes
Explanation: 'Exposure' for this purpose shall include all fund based and non-fund
based (credit as well as investment) exposure of banks / FIs to the borrower.
requirements.object: with aggregate exposure of ₹ 5 crore and above from banks (Scheduled Commercial Banks (excluding Regional Rural B

requirements.full_sentence:
Aggregate sanctioned limit or outstanding balance, whichever is higher, shall be
reckoned for the purpose.
requirements.object: purpose

requirements.full_sentence:
Lenders shall ascertain the position of aggregate
exposure based on information available either with them, or Central Repository of
Information on Large Credits (CRILC) database or declaration obtained from the
borrower
26.
requirements.object: aggregate exposure based on information available either with them, or Central Repository of Information on Large Credits (C

requirements.full_sentence:
Borrowers who fail to obtain LEI codes from an authorised Local Operating Unit
(LOU) shall not be sanctioned any new exposure nor shall they be granted renewal
/ enhancement of any existing exposure.
requirements.object: exposure nor shall they be granted renewal / enhancement of any existing exposure

requirements.full_sentence:
However, Departments / Agencies of
Central and State Governments (not Public Sector Undertakings registered under
Companies Act or established as Corporation under the relevant statute) shall be
exempted from this provision.
requirements.object: provision

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall encourage large borrowers to obtain LEI for their parent entity as well as
all subsidiaries and associates.
requirements.object: obtain LEI for their parent entity as well as all subsidiaries and associates

requirements.full_sentence: In India, LEI code may be obtained

A bank shall be guided by the following aspects while formulating a policy on
valuation of properties and appointment of valuers.
requirements.object: following aspects while formulating a policy on valuation of properties and appointment of valuers

requirements.full_sentence:
Policy for valuation of properties
(1) A bank shall have a Board approved policy in place for valuation of properties
including collaterals accepted for their exposures.
requirements.object: policy in place for valuation of properties including collaterals accepted for their exposures

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The valuation shall be done by professionally qualified independent valuers
i.e.
requirements.object: qualified independent valuers i

requirements.full_sentence: the valuer shall not have a direct or indirect interest.
requirements.object: indirect interest

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) A bank shall obtain minimum two Independent Valuation Reports for properties
valued at ₹50 crore or above.
requirements.object: Valuation Reports for properties valued at ₹50 crore or above

requirements.full_sentence:
Revaluation of bank's own properties: In addition to the above, a bank may keep the
following aspects in view while formulating policy for revaluation of their own
properties:
(1) As banks are permitted to include revaluation reserves as part of Capital in terms
of Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital
Adequacy) Directions, 2025, it is necessary that revaluation reserves represent
true appreciation in the market value of the properties and banks have in place a
comprehensive policy for revaluation of fixed assets owned by them.
requirements.object: in view while formulating policy for revaluation of their own properties: (1) As banks are permitted to include revaluation

requirements.full_sentence:
Such a
policy shall inter alia cover procedure for identification of assets for revaluation,
maintenance of separate set of records for such assets, the frequency of
revaluation, depreciation policy for such assets, policy for sale of such revalued
assets etc.
requirements.object: for identification of assets for revaluation, maintenance of separate set of records for such assets, the frequency of reva

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall also cover the disclosure required to be made in the
'Notes on Account' regarding the details of revaluation such as the original cost
of the fixed assets subject to revaluation and accounting treatment for
appreciation / depreciation etc.
requirements.object: required to be made in the 'Notes on Account' regarding the details of revaluation such as the original cost of the fixed as

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) As the revaluation shall reflect the change in the fair value of the fixed asset, the
frequency of revaluation shall be determined based on the observed volatility in
the prices of the assets in the past.
requirements.object: the fair value of the fixed asset, the frequency of revaluation shall be determined based on the observed volatility in the pric

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, any change in the method of
depreciation shall reflect the change in the expected pattern of consumption of
<image>
the future economic benefits of the assets.
requirements.object: the expected pattern of consumption of <image> the future economic benefits of the assets

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall adhere to these principles
meticulously while changing the frequency of revaluation / method of depreciation
for a particular class of asset and shall make proper disclosures in this regard.
requirements.object: meticulously while changing the frequency of revaluation / method of depreciation for a particular class of asset and shall

requirements.full_sentence:
Policy for Empanelment of Independent valuers
(1) A bank shall have a procedure for empanelment of professional valuers and
maintain a register / record of 'approved list of valuers'.
requirements.object: empanelment of professional valuers and maintain a register / record of 'approved list of valuers'

requirements.full_sentence: (2) A bank may prescribe a minimum qualification for empanelment of valuers.
requirements.object: for empanelment of valuers

requirements.full_sentence: Different qualifications may be prescribed for different classes of assets (e.g.
requirements.object: classes of assets (e

requirements.full_sentence:
While prescribing the
qualification, the bank shall take into consideration the qualifications prescribed
under Section 34AB (Rule 8A) of the Wealth Tax Act, 1957.
requirements.object: qualifications prescribed under Section 34AB (Rule 8A) of the Wealth Tax Act, 1957

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall also be guided by the relevant Accounting Standard issued by the
Institute of Chartered Accountants of India.
requirements.object: the relevant Accounting Standard issued by the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India

requirements.full_sentence:
The records maintained by the Central Registry shall be available for search by
any lender or any other person desirous of dealing with the property.
requirements.object: by any lender or any other person desirous of dealing with the property

requirements.full_sentence:
It may be noted that under the provisions of
Section 23 of the SARFAESI Act, every transaction of security interest is required
to be filed with the Registry.
requirements.object: the provisions of Section 23 of the SARFAESI Act, every transaction of security interest is required to be filed with the Registry

requirements.full_sentence:
Current Accounts, Cash Credit Accounts (CC), and Overdraft Accounts (OD) may
all be used as transaction accounts by the customers, which raises concerns relating
to credit monitoring by the lenders.
requirements.object: transaction accounts by the customers, which raises concerns relating to credit monitoring by the lenders

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation (1): 'Banking System' for the purpose of this Chapter shall include
Commercial Banks (including Small Finance Banks, Local Area Banks, and Regional
Rural Banks, but excluding Payments Banks), Urban Co-operative Banks and Rural
Co-operative Banks (State Co-operative Banks and Central Co-operative Banks).
requirements.object: Small Finance Banks, Local Area Banks, and Regional Rural Banks, but excluding Payments Banks), Urban Co-operative

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation (2): 'Exposure' for the purpose of this Chapter means the sum of all
sanctioned fund-based credit facilities and non-fund-based facilities availed by the
borrower from the banking system.
requirements.object: sanctioned fund-based credit facilities and non-fund-based facilities availed by the borrower from the banking system

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: 'Collection Account' for the purpose of this Chapter means a current
account or OD account used primarily for receipts of cash inflows of the
accountholder.
requirements.object: OD account used primarily for receipts of cash inflows of the accountholder

requirements.full_sentence:
Restricted payments / cash outflows from such account shall be
subject to the conditions outlined in paragraph 38F of these Directions.
requirements.object: conditions outlined in paragraph 38F of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
With a view to ensuring credit discipline, lenders may include additional covenants
as per their policies in their loan agreements in mutual agreement with borrowers.
requirements.object: per their policies in their loan agreements in mutual agreement with borrowers

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may provide such cash credit facilities as per the needs of the
customer, without any restriction under this Chapter.
requirements.object: facilities as per the needs of the customer, without any restriction under this Chapter

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may maintain current account or OD account without any restriction in case
of borrowers where the aggregate exposure of the banking system to the customer is
less than ₹10 crore.
requirements.object: OD account without any restriction in case of borrowers where the aggregate exposure of the banking system to the customer

requirements.full_sentence:
Funds credited into a collection account shall be remitted within two working days
of receipt of such funds to a CC account, current account, or OD account maintained
with any bank in the banking system and designated by the borrower for this purpose
<image>
(hereinafter referred to as 'designated account' in this Chapter).
requirements.object: working days of receipt of such funds to a CC account, current account, or OD account maintained with any bank in the banking s

requirements.full_sentence:
Any disbursement of
overdraft limit from an OD account, which is in the nature of a collection account, shall
be through the designated account only.
requirements.object: account only

conditions: Provided that statutory dues such as taxes
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that statutory dues such as taxes, and dues, if any, to the bank maintaining
the collection account may be debited before remitting the funds.
requirements.object: taxes, and dues

requirements.full_sentence:
The restrictions placed in terms of paragraph 38D(1) of these Directions shall not
be applicable to the accounts mentioned below:
(1) Accounts opened as per the provisions of Foreign Exchange Management Act,
1999 (FEMA) and notifications issued thereunder, including accounts mandated
for ensuring compliance under the FEMA framework.
requirements.object: accounts mentioned below: (1) Accounts opened as per the provisions of Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (FEMA) and notificat

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that banks operating the above-mentioned exempted accounts shall
ensure that transactions in such accounts are used only for the permitted /
specified purposes.
requirements.object: such accounts are used only for the permitted / specified purposes

requirements.full_sentence:
Surplus funds, if any, in such accounts shall be remitted to
the designated account.
requirements.object: designated account

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of ensuring ongoing compliance with this Chapter, all banks shall
monitor accounts maintained with them on a regular basis, and in any case at least
once every half-year.
requirements.object: them on a regular basis, and in any case at least once every half-year

requirements.full_sentence:
then the bank shall notify the customer(s) concerned promptly, and in any case within
one month from the date of observing such ineligibility, that the account must either
be converted to a collection account or closed.
requirements.object: promptly, and in any case within one month from the date of observing such ineligibility, that the account must eithe

requirements.full_sentence:
The conversion or closure process, as
the case may be, shall be completed within three months of observing such
ineligibility.
requirements.object: months of observing such ineligibility

requirements.full_sentence:
Accounts opened in terms of these Directions shall be appropriately flagged in the
bank's core banking solution (CBS) to ensure clear identification and to facilitate
effective monitoring.
requirements.object: the bank's core banking solution (CBS) to ensure clear identification and to facilitate effective monitoring

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks maintaining multiple accounts for a borrower shall ensure
that such accounts and transactions and cashflows therein are monitored at the
borrower level as also at the account level.
requirements.object: and transactions and cashflows therein are monitored at the borrower level as also at the account level

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that an accountholder utilise their account solely for
transactions related to their authorised business or activities.
requirements.object: utilise their account solely for transactions related to their authorised business or activities

requirements.full_sentence:
These accounts shall
not, under any circumstances, be used as pass-through channels for facilitating third-
party transactions.
requirements.object: be used as pass-through channels for facilitating third- party transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that entities expressly licensed or authorised by a financial sector regulator
to facilitate third-party transactions may continue to do so.
requirements.object: authorised by a financial sector regulator to facilitate third-party transactions may continue to do so

requirements.full_sentence:
However, such activities
shall strictly be limited to the specific transactions they are authorised to do and shall
not extend beyond that scope.
requirements.object: the specific transactions they are authorised to do and shall not extend beyond that scope

requirements.full_sentence:
Any account that has been permitted to carry out such
third-party transactions shall be appropriately flagged in the bank's CBS to ensure
clear identification and to facilitate effective monitoring.
requirements.object: the bank's CBS to ensure clear identification and to facilitate effective monitoring

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall ensure that an accountholder, who is not licensed or authorised by
the Reserve Bank to accept deposits or to provide payment services, do not engage
in such activities through accounts maintained with them.
requirements.object: who is not licensed or authorised by the Reserve Bank to accept deposits or to provide payment services, do not engage i

requirements.full_sentence:
Robust monitoring systems shall be implemented to detect the above prohibited
usage, including mechanisms to flag accounts exhibiting unusually high transaction
volumes, frequent pass-through activities, or inconsistencies between the
accountholder's stated line of business and transactions carried out through the
account.
requirements.object: the above prohibited usage, including mechanisms to flag accounts exhibiting unusually high transaction volumes, frequent pas

requirements.full_sentence:
Term loans sanctioned by the bank shall preferably be remitted directly to the
intended beneficiary's account(s) or for the specified end-use, where such beneficiary
is identifiable, rather than routing the funds through the borrower's account.]
<image>
requirements.object: to the intended beneficiary's account(s) or for the specified end-use

requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions, instructions and
guidelines already repealed shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to
be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or acknowledgments granted
under these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions
affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being
in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
<image>
Directions, the Reserve Bank may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any
provision of these Directions given by the Reserve Bank shall be final and binding.

These losses may reduce their
capacity to service the loans taken from the banking system and increase their
probability of default thereby affecting the health of the banking system.
requirements.object: service the loans taken from the banking system and increase their probability of default thereby affecting the health of the

requirements.full_sentence:
The aim of the framework was that the banks shall price the risk from Unhedged
Foreign Currency Exposure (UFCE) as credit risk premium which may nudge entities
to hedge their foreign currency exposures in the market.
requirements.object: Unhedged Foreign Currency Exposure (UFCE) as credit risk premium which may nudge entities to hedge their foreign currency exposure
